title: Living in Boulder vlog 1 -  22 - 2017: Google Earthing Roman Colosseum.  A real mind fuck.

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs at 10am after pulling an all-nighter, describing his ongoing project of building a detailed 3D model of the Roman Colosseum in Google Earth using the polygon tool. He explains the complex multi-level stair system of the Colosseum, critiques existing models (including Google's own) for being inaccurate, and expresses ambition to be the first to accurately illustrate how the structure was designed and how visitors moved through it.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Most are misleading and quite incorrect, including Google Earth's version
More complicated and ambitious than expected, but a satisfying mental challenge right at his level
Excited to be the one to explain how it was designed since nobody else has done it accurately

claims.claim: I feel like it's January 23rd, 2018, and it's 10 out of 6 in the morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I never went to sleep last night, but I did sleep like all day today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My mom just called me and I'm supposed to go to her house to help her organize stuff in an hour.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The last time I did a video, I was working on the Colosseum, specifically the stairs or something.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been staring at the mats for the Colosseum over the last few days because the design seems complicated.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: A lot of the models of the Colosseum that exist now are misleading and incorrect.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I downloaded a YouTube video that guides through the Colosseum scene with a video game like Warcraft.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: Nobody has written a comic model of the Colosseum, and I hope my version looks good.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm building the Colosseum model using the polygon tool on Google Earth.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: The map I have now shows only the lower couple levels, and I made stairs that go to the third level.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: There are stairs that go from the third level to the third level, and I have to make stairs go all around the Colosseum.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: The Colosseum has five levels, and I have to make stairs that go from the second to the third level, and then from the third to the fourth, and so on.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: The Colosseum is 48 meters high, and when you're on the almost second level, that's only 20 meters up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to make a movie of traveling all the way up the flights of stairs and through the Colosseum.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: Google Earth made the Colosseum model incorrectly, as it doesn't show the stairs correctly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I watched a YouTube channel called Virtual Tours where a dude walks around attractions like the Colosseum.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: The way Google Earth has the Colosseum model is incorrect because it doesn't show the stairs going up higher.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I figured out how to design the Colosseum model realistically and make it easy to see how it works.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might have to call the doctor because my scar from skin cancer acted up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm excited to try to explain how the Colosseum was designed because it's complicated and nobody shows it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Rebuilding the Colosseum is a good mental challenge that's hard but not too hard, right at my level.
